title: Multi-step form vs single-page form vs single-question page vs step navigation
summary: Use a multi-step form when a transaction needs several saved pages or sections before a final review and submission.
decisionRules: Prefer a multi-step form when a transaction has several ordered pages, sections, or question groups that cannot be reliably completed and recovered on one page.
Prefer a single-page form when a compact related field set should be scanned, edited, validated, and submitted together.
Prefer a single-question page when the current page asks exactly one answer or decision and may be one page inside a larger multi-step form.
Use step navigation only as progress communication inside a multi-step form; it is not the transaction, validation model, or submission contract by itself.
Include a review or check-answers step when users need a final chance to confirm captured answers before the transaction is submitted.
Save or preserve each step's answers before users move forward, backward, refresh, or return from the review step.
Do not use a visible step indicator when the flow has fewer than three meaningful sections or when the number of steps changes heavily based on answers.
Do not let users jump to future steps until prerequisites are complete unless the form is explicitly designed for non-linear task completion.
When an earlier answer changes, recompute later steps and make stale downstream answers visible before final submission.
Keep each step's page title, progress text, Back and Continue behavior, validation, and stored state synchronized with the route.
failureModes: A multi-step form marks a step complete because it was visited, even though required fields were not saved.
Back or browser refresh clears answers from earlier steps.
A step indicator shows a fixed count while conditional answers insert or remove pages later.
Review links return users to an earlier page but force them through the whole transaction again.
An earlier eligibility answer changes and stale upload or payment answers remain silently valid.
The flow uses wizard-style chrome for a short single-page edit task.

sources.note: GOV.UK form-structure guidance supports splitting forms across pages, one thing per page, saving as users go, and handling branching questions and loops.
GOV.UK check-answers guidance supports review before submission, change links, pre-populated return pages, and returning users to review without repeating the whole flow.
USWDS step indicator guidance supports progress display for linear multi-page forms with three or more high-level steps.
